fuzzing_required.description: API endpoints with rich input surface need aggressive parameter fuzzing for injection discovery
suggested_actions: Run ffuf -w params.txt:PARAM -w payloads.txt:VAL on all endpoints with -mc 200,201,302,500
Test ALL string params simultaneously with: ' " <script>alert(1)</script> {{7*7}} ../../../etc/passwd
Fuzz HTTP methods: try PATCH/PUT/DELETE/CONNECT/TRACE on POST-only and GET-only endpoints

race_condition_likely.description: Financial/state-changing endpoints — race condition potential for double-spend or state corruption
suggested_actions: Send 50 simultaneous requests with same idempotency key — check if action applied multiple times
Turbo Intruder gate: prepare all requests then release simultaneously with last-byte sync technique
Compare final state vs expected: 50 concurrent withdrawals of $1 should not exceed account balance

idor_hotspot.description: Object reference parameters (IDs) detected — IDOR highly likely across user/resource boundaries
suggested_actions: Replace your user_id with adjacent IDs (id-1, id+1, id+100) — check for other users data in response
Create two test accounts, capture account1 object IDs, access them from account2 authenticated session
Vertical escalation: use regular user session to access admin-owned object IDs (1, 2, 3, 999)

creative_bypass_needed.description: Security controls detected — WAF/filter bypass techniques required for exploit confirmation
suggested_actions: Encoding bypass: URL-encode, double-encode, Unicode normalize, hex-encode payloads to bypass WAF signatures
Case/whitespace: <ScRiPt>, null bytes %00, tab %09/newline %0a as whitespace alternatives in payloads
Split payload across multiple parameters or chunked Transfer-Encoding to evade single-field inspection

api_versioning_exposure.description: Multiple API versions active — older versions often lack security controls implemented in current version
suggested_actions: If /api/v2/users has auth, test /api/v1/users and /api/v0/users — older versions often lack auth middleware
Probe /api/beta/, /api/internal/, /api/dev/, /api/test/ — staging endpoints may bypass production auth
Compare response schemas between versions — v1 may return extra sensitive fields stripped in v2
